Decentralized Safe Path Following for Multi-Quadrotor Systems

Safety-critical decentralized control for multiple quadrotors on intersecting sinusoidal and circular paths

View Code on GitHub

Overview animation of the decentralized multi-quadrotor path-following simulations.

Abstract

This project presents a decentralized safe path-following framework for multiple quadrotors moving on intersecting trajectories. Each quadrotor solves its own quadratic program online to satisfy path-following objectives while enforcing safety-critical constraints. The controller explicitly accounts for roll and pitch safety through control barrier function constraints and guarantees inter-agent collision avoidance through pairwise safety constraints. The resulting framework enables multiple vehicles to remain on their assigned paths, negotiate intersections safely, and maintain stable flight without requiring centralized trajectory optimization.

What this project demonstrates

The repository focuses on decentralized closed-loop simulations of multi-quadrotor systems in challenging shared airspace. In particular, the simulations highlight:

  • safe path following on intersecting sinusoidal paths
  • safe path following on overlapping circular paths
  • decentralized online control, with a per-agent quadratic program
  • safety enforcement for both attitude limits and pairwise collision avoidance

Rather than treating the path as a precomputed trajectory to be rigidly tracked in time, the controller drives each vehicle to remain on its geometric path while advancing safely along it.

Simulation environments

1. Intersecting sinusoidal paths

In this environment, quadrotors move on sinusoidal paths that intersect spatially. This creates repeated proximity events where vehicles must remain on their assigned paths while also respecting collision-avoidance constraints. The sinusoidal setting is useful for visualizing how the controller handles path curvature, path crossings, and distributed conflict resolution.

XY view: top-down motion on intersecting sinusoidal paths.

3D view: spatial evolution of the same sinusoidal-path scenario.

2. Intersecting circular paths

In the circular-path environment, multiple quadrotors follow circular orbits with centers offset from one another. This produces recurring close encounters and intersection regions where decentralized safety constraints are repeatedly activated. The circular setting highlights continuous path progression, repeated interaction patterns, and robust maintenance of safe spacing over long horizons.

XY view: top-down motion on intersecting circular paths.

3D view: multi-quadrotor circular-path simulation in space.

What the animations show

Each animation illustrates a different aspect of the controller:

  • Top-down XY animations emphasize geometric path following and inter-agent separation in the plane.
  • 3D animations show the full vehicle motion and provide a clearer view of spatial coordination.
  • Moving vehicle renderings highlight the local attitude and heading of each quadrotor as it progresses along the path.
  • Finite trailing history makes it easier to read the recent motion of each agent without cluttering the scene with the entire trajectory.

Together, these views show that the quadrotors stay close to their desired paths while safely negotiating intersections and shared regions.

Controller structure

The implementation combines:

  • path-following objectives in transformed coordinates
  • roll and pitch safety constraints using control barrier functions
  • pairwise collision-avoidance constraints between neighboring agents
  • a decentralized quadratic program solved independently for each quadrotor at every time step

This structure allows each vehicle to make safety-aware control decisions using the current state of the multi-agent system while avoiding centralized trajectory coordination.

Repository contents

The repository includes code for:

  • quadrotor dynamics simulation
  • decentralized safe path-following control
  • pairwise safety constraints for collision avoidance
  • visualization utilities for 2D and 3D plots
  • animated GIF generation for sinusoidal and circular multi-agent scenarios

Requirements

```bash pip install numpy cvxpy matplotlib
